commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6c780f9be643112494c914c2566dbd6fe6746918)
commands.review_summary:
Adds an exec approval follow-up suppression diagnostic event at direct-delivery and gateway-preflight stale drop paths, with public diagnostic stream and stability recorder tests.

PR surface: Source +35, Tests +89. Total +124 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main has stale exec approval follow-up suppression branches in the agents and gateway paths that only log or return an ok dropped summary. I did not run a live approval-timeout scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Author or maintainer action is needed to fix the stability bundle round trip and provide real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor’s real setup proof.

target.title: fix(gateway): emit stale exec approval followup diagnostics
target.body:
Related: #98279
Alternative to #98292 with the gateway preflight suppression point and public/stability diagnostics covered.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where operators could not consume a machine-readable signal when OpenClaw intentionally suppressed a stale exec approval follow-up after the target session was rebound by `/new` or `/reset`.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a structured `exec.approval.followup_suppressed` diagnostic event at the existing stale-follow-up drop points without changing delivery behavior. The event includes the approval id, a stable `session_rebound` reason, and the suppression phase while avoiding raw session keys, route ids, command text, or other high-cardinality routing details.

## User Impact

Operator status and inbox tooling can now distinguish an intentional stale approval follow-up suppression from missing channel delivery or a stuck approval path. Existing Slack/direct/gateway suppression behavior remains unchanged; this is additive observability only.

AI-assisted with Codex.

## Evidence

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-followup.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stability.test.ts` -> 4 Vitest shards passed: gateway 344 tests, infra 30 tests, logging 12 tests, agents 27 tests.

Coverage added:
- Direct stale denied follow-up suppression emits `exec.approval.followup_suppressed`.
- Gateway preflight stale follow-up suppression emits the same event before touching the rebound session.
- Public diagnostic subscribers receive the event.
- Diagnostic stability records approval id, reason, and phase for operator tooling.

Note: a fresh `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` was attempted twice in this new shallow checkout. Both attempts timed out on registry downloads for a small set of native/package tarballs, but the partial install hydrated enough for the focused repo test wrapper to run and pass the acceptance bundle above.
target.author: bsniznd
